stage.name: What is DeFi? (Elementary)
stage.description: Banking without banks — explained for complete beginners.

elements.content: DeFi stands for Decentralized Finance. It's a way to do banking stuff — saving, borrowing, earning interest — without using a real bank. Instead of people running it, computer programs (called smart contracts) do everything automatically. No waiting, no paperwork, no business hours.

🌍

No Approval

Anyone can use it — no application

actions.text: 🏗️ What is DeFi?
Banking without banks — powered by code

DeFi stands for Decentralized Finance. It's a way to do banking stuff — saving, borrowing, earning interest — without using a real bank. Instead of people running it, computer programs (called smart contracts) do everything automatically. No waiting, no paperwork, no business hours
🕐Open 24/7/365Never closed — no holidays
🌍No ApprovalAnyone can use it — no application
⚡InstantTransfers happen in seconds
🤖AutomatedCode handles everything automatically

elements.content: Like a vending machine — put money in, get exactly what was promised

A smart contract is a program on the blockchain. When certain conditions are met, it automatically does something — no human needed. Think of a vending machine: put in money, press a button, get your snack. No cashier. No waiting. The machine just does it.

💰

Deposit Crypto

You put in $100 of ETH as collateral

✅

Pay Back

Return it + fee, get ETH back auto

actions.text: 🤖 What is a Smart Contract?
Like a vending machine — put money in, get exactly what was promised

A smart contract is a program on the blockchain. When certain conditions are met, it automatically does something — no human needed. Think of a vending machine: put in money, press a button, get your snack. No cashier. No waiting. The machine just does it
💰Deposit CryptoYou put in $100 of ETH as collateral
🤖Auto LendContract lends you $70 instantly
✅Pay BackReturn it + fee, get ETH back auto
🏦No HumanZero bank employees involved

elements.content: What it actually means to remove the middleman

Every time you use a bank — to send money, take a loan, or earn interest — the bank is the middleman. It charges fees, sets the rules, and can freeze your account.


DeFi replaces the bank with code. Smart contracts handle lending, borrowing, and trading automatically. No human approvals. No business hours. No border restrictions. Anyone with a crypto wallet can participate.

🚫 No Bank
No middleman, no fees

📋 Code Rules
Smart contracts are the law

actions.text: 🏦 Finance Without Banks
What it actually means to remove the middleman

Every time you use a bank — to send money, take a loan, or earn interest — the bank is the middleman. It charges fees, sets the rules, and can freeze your account. DeFi replaces the bank with code. Smart contracts handle lending, borrowing, and trading automatically. No human approvals. No business hours. No border restrictions. Anyone with a crypto wallet can participate
🚫 No Bank. No middleman, no fees
⏰ 24/7. Open every day, always
🌍 Global. Anyone, anywhere
📋 Code Rules. Smart contracts are the law

elements.content:
Yield means earning a return on your crypto just for providing it to a DeFi protocol. Similar to earning interest in a savings account — but often much higher rates.


For example: you deposit USDC into a lending protocol. Borrowers pay interest to borrow it. That interest flows back to you automatically via smart contract. Your crypto works for you while you sleep.

💵 Deposit Crypto
Lock it in a protocol

💸 You Earn
Interest flows back to you

actions.text: 💰 What is Yield?
Earning passive income with your crypto

Yield means earning a return on your crypto just for providing it to a DeFi protocol. Similar to earning interest in a savings account — but often much higher rates. For example: you deposit USDC into a lending protocol. Borrowers pay interest to borrow it. That interest flows back to you automatically via smart contract. Your crypto works for you while you sleep
💵 Deposit Crypto. Lock it in a protocol
📤 Borrowers Use It. They pay interest
💸 You Earn. Interest flows back to you
⚠️ Risk. Smart contracts can have bugs

elements.content: How DeFi creates markets without order books

Traditional exchanges match buyers and sellers. DeFi uses a different system called a liquidity pool.


Imagine a giant jar filled with two types of tokens — say, ETH and USDC. When you want to swap ETH for USDC, you add your ETH to the jar and take out USDC. The price adjusts automatically based on the ratio in the jar. The people who filled the jar earn a small fee on every swap.


⚠️ Risk: Rug Pulls. If the team behind a pool withdraws all the liquidity, the value collapses instantly. Only use established, audited protocols.

actions.text: 🌊 Liquidity Pools — The Concept
How DeFi creates markets without order books

Traditional exchanges match buyers and sellers. DeFi uses a different system called a liquidity pool. Imagine a giant jar filled with two types of tokens — say, ETH and USDC. When you want to swap ETH for USDC, you add your ETH to the jar and take out USDC. The price adjusts automatically based on the ratio in the jar. The people who filled the jar earn a small fee on every swap. ⚠️ Risk: Rug Pulls. If the team behind a pool withdraws all the liquidity, the value collapses instantly. Only use established, audited protocols
🫙 Pool. Jar of two token types
🔄 Swap. Trade against the pool
💰 LP Fee. Earn % of every swap
🚨 Rug Pull. Team drains the pool

questions.question: What does DeFi stand for?
questions.analysis: DeFi stands for Decentralized Finance — financial services that run on blockchains without banks or other middlemen.

questions.question: What is a smart contract?

options.label: A program on the blockchain that automatically executes when conditions are met
A bank account with smart features

questions.analysis: A smart contract is code on the blockchain that runs automatically when certain conditions are met — like a vending machine that gives you a snack the moment you put in the right amount.

questions.question: Which is an advantage of DeFi over a traditional bank?

options.label: Anyone can access it 24/7 with no approval needed

questions.analysis: DeFi is open to everyone, 24 hours a day, 7 days a week. You don't need a bank account, a credit score, or anyone's approval.

questions.question: How does OnlyCrypto's payout system relate to DeFi concepts?

options.label: Payouts are approved manually by staff
Payouts happen automatically without anyone approving them — similar to smart contracts
Members must go to a bank to receive payouts

questions.analysis: OnlyCrypto's payouts work like DeFi — they happen automatically using the XRP Ledger. When you earn a payout, the system sends XRP to your wallet without anyone manually approving it.

actions.text: Time to test what you've learned! Answer each question and see how you do.
Take your time — the goal is understanding, not just getting the right answer.
